_source: Legal practice guides, Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, Contract law casebooks
_note: Long-tail legal knowledge. Contract drafting, litigation procedures, legal research methods.

essential_elements.offer: Definite proposal; specific terms; communicated to offeree; 'I will sell you X for $Y'
essential_elements.acceptance: Unqualified agreement to exact terms; mirror image rule (common law); UCC allows modifications
essential_elements.consideration: Bargained-for exchange; each party gives something of value; past consideration doesn't count
essential_elements.capacity: Legal ability to contract; 18+ years; mentally competent; not under duress/undue influence
essential_elements.legality: Lawful purpose; illegal contracts void (gambling in most states, contracts to commit crimes)

statute_of_frauds.writing_requirement: Material terms identified; signed by party to be charged; doesn't need to be formal contract
statute_of_frauds.exceptions: Partial performance; merchant's confirmatory memo; admission in pleadings

key_clauses.recitals: 'WHEREAS' clauses; background and purpose; not binding but aids interpretation
key_clauses.definitions: Define ambiguous terms; 'Software means the Licensed Software described in Exhibit A'; capitalized when used
key_clauses.payment_terms: Amount, schedule, method; late fees; 'Net 30 means payment due within 30 days of invoice date'

term_and_termination.term: 1 year from Effective Date; auto-renew for successive 1-year terms
term_and_termination.termination_for_convenience: Either party may terminate with 30 days written notice
term_and_termination.termination_for_cause: Material breach; 15-day cure period; immediate termination if not cured

warranties.express: Seller warrants Software will perform substantially as described for 90 days
warranties.disclaimer: EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY PROVIDED, SOFTWARE PROVIDED 'AS IS' WITHOUT WARRANTIES

limitation_of_liability.cap: IN NO EVENT SHALL TOTAL LIABILITY EXCEED AMOUNTS PAID IN PRIOR 12 MONTHS
limitation_of_liability.carveouts: Except for breach of confidentiality, IP infringement, gross negligence

key_clauses.indemnification: Party A shall defend, indemnify, hold harmless Party B from third-party claims arising from A's breach
key_clauses.confidentiality: Non-disclosure of proprietary information; exceptions: public domain, independently developed, required by law
key_clauses.governing_law: This Agreement shall be governed by laws of State of Delaware without regard to conflicts of law

dispute_resolution.arbitration: Binding arbitration under AAA rules; waives right to jury trial; limits appeals
dispute_resolution.jurisdiction_venue: Exclusive jurisdiction in courts of New York County; consent to personal jurisdiction

key_clauses.entire_agreement: This Agreement constitutes entire understanding; supersedes prior agreements; amendments must be in writing
key_clauses.severability: If any provision held invalid, remaining provisions remain in effect
key_clauses.force_majeure: Excuses performance for events beyond control (war, natural disaster, pandemic)

interpretation_principles.plain_meaning: Words given ordinary meaning unless defined otherwise
interpretation_principles.contra_proferentem: Ambiguities construed against drafter; reason to be precise
interpretation_principles.ejusdem_generis: 'Cars, trucks, and other vehicles' - 'vehicles' limited to similar items (not planes)
interpretation_principles.entire_agreement_clause: Parol evidence rule; prior negotiations inadmissible to contradict written agreement

pre_litigation.demand_letter: Outline claim; damages sought; deadline to respond; settlement demand; preserves claim record

statute_of_limitations.contract: 4-6 years (varies by state); written vs oral
statute_of_limitations.personal_injury: 2-3 years; discovery rule (when injury discovered) vs occurrence
statute_of_limitations.fraud: 2-6 years; often from discovery of fraud
statute_of_limitations.calculation: Claim accrues when cause of action complete; breach of contract = date of breach

complaint.caption: Court, parties, case number, title
complaint.jurisdiction_venue: Basis for subject matter jurisdiction (diversity >$75k, federal question) and venue
complaint.statement_of_claim: Numbered paragraphs; factual allegations; short and plain statement
complaint.prayer_for_relief: Damages sought (specify amount if required); equitable relief; costs and fees
complaint.rule_11: Attorney certifies factual/legal basis; sanctions for frivolous filing

answer.due: 21 days after service (FRCP 12); 60 days if waiver of service
answer.admit_deny: Each paragraph admitted, denied, or insufficient knowledge (treated as denial)
answer.affirmative_defenses: Must be pled or waived: statute of limitations, accord and satisfaction, assumption of risk, failure to state claim

counterclaim.compulsory: Arises from same transaction/occurrence; must be filed or waived
counterclaim.permissive: Unrelated claim; may be filed separately

interrogatories.response_time: 30 days; objections must be specific; can't object 'overly broad' without more
interrogatories.use: Identify witnesses, documents, factual contentions; subject to attorney-client privilege

document_requests.scope: Relevant and proportional to needs of case; need not be admissible
document_requests.production: 30 days; organize as kept in ordinary course or labeled to correspond to request
document_requests.privilege_log: List withheld documents; identify privilege; author, recipient, date, description

depositions.notice: 30 days; subpoena if non-party; corporate representative (30(b)(6)) designated by organization
depositions.conduct: Sworn testimony; recorded; objections preserved; defending counsel can instruct not to answer (privilege, harassment)
depositions.use: Impeachment at trial; summary judgment; admissible if witness unavailable

privilege.attorney_client: Confidential communication for legal advice; client holds privilege; waived if disclosed to third party
privilege.work_product: Materials prepared in anticipation of litigation; opinion work product nearly absolute; fact work product if substantial need and undue hardship

summary_judgment.standard: No genuine dispute of material fact; entitled to judgment as matter of law (FRCP 56)
summary_judgment.timing: 30 days after close of discovery (default); movant can file earlier
summary_judgment.burden: Movant shows no genuine dispute; non-movant must come forward with evidence creating dispute
summary_judgment.evidence: Affidavits, deposition testimony, documents; personal knowledge; admissible form; no conclusory statements
summary_judgment.viewed_in_light: All evidence and inferences viewed in light most favorable to non-movant

trial.jury_demand: Within 14 days of last pleading; waived if not timely; right to jury for legal claims (damages) not equitable (injunction)
trial.voir_dire: Jury selection; challenges for cause (unlimited); peremptory challenges (limited; can't be race/gender-based)
trial.opening_statements: Roadmap of evidence; not argument; plaintiff first
trial.burden_of_proof: Preponderance of evidence (>50%); plaintiff has burden; defendant burden on affirmative defenses
trial.direct_examination: Open-ended questions; can't lead your own witness (except preliminary, hostile witness)
trial.cross_examination: Leading questions allowed; limited to scope of direct; impeachment allowed
trial.closing_argument: Draw inferences; argue credibility; apply law to facts; plaintiff goes first and has rebuttal
trial.jury_instructions: Submitted in advance; conference with judge; given before or after closing
trial.verdict: Unanimous in federal (unless stipulated); majority in some states; special verdict form (specific findings) vs general verdict

post_trial.judgment_notwithstanding_verdict: JNOV; no reasonable jury could have found for prevailing party; rarely granted
post_trial.new_trial: Motion within 28 days; errors in trial; verdict against weight of evidence; excessive damages
post_trial.appeal: Notice within 30 days of judgment; appellate court reviews law de novo, facts for clear error; abuse of discretion on evidentiary rulings

primary_authority.statutes: Enacted by legislature; organized by code (U.S.C., state codes)
primary_authority.regulations: Administrative agencies; C.F.R. (federal), state regs; notice and comment rulemaking
primary_authority.case_law: Court decisions; binding (mandatory) vs persuasive (other jurisdictions)

hierarchy.federal: Supreme Court > Circuit Court of Appeals > District Court
hierarchy.binding: Lower courts bound by higher courts in same jurisdiction; not bound by other circuits (persuasive only)
hierarchy.state: State Supreme Court > Appellate Courts > Trial Courts; not bound by federal courts except on federal questions

research_process.identify_issue: What is the legal question? Jurisdiction? Area of law?
research_process.secondary_sources: Law review articles, treatises, restatements; get overview; find key cases
research_process.primary_sources: Read statutes and cases; note citations; shepardize/keycite for validity

updating.shepards_keycite: Case still good law? Overruled? Distinguished? Criticized?
updating.red_flag: Negative treatment; yellow flag = caution; green = positive

elements.facts: Relevant facts giving rise to dispute
elements.procedural_history: Lower court decisions; how case arrived at this court
elements.issue: Legal question before court; framed as yes/no
elements.holding: Court's answer to issue; rule of law
elements.reasoning: Court's analysis; policy considerations; application of law to facts
